nodes.title: Prompting LTX-2
widgets_values:
## LTX-2 Prompting Tips
1. **Core Actions**: Describe events and actions as they occur over time  
2. **Audio**: Describe sounds and dialogue needed for the scene  
3. **Reference Image**: Do not repeat details already present  
4. **Consistency**: Avoid instructions that do not match the reference image, as this will degrade results

nodes.title: About  Models
widgets_values:
Download models from here:


https://huggingface.co/Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy

Text encoder : https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/ltx-2

nodes.title: User Made Loras
widgets_values: If using some user made LTX-2 loras they sometimes are not trained on audio, so it will produce very noisy audio outputs. Try use KJNodes LTX-2 Lora Loader Advanced in such cases, and set the non video strenght to zero

nodes.title: About Size
widgets_values:
Width & height settings must be divisible by 32 + 1. 
Frame count must be divisible by 8 + 1. 


Running with invalid parameters **will not cause errors**. Instead, the flow will silently choose the closest valid parameters. 

By default, we are using 720p resolution. You can try 1920*1088 if you have a powerful GPU.

nodes.title: Sampler Preview
widgets_values:
Tiny VAE for sampler previews. 
Optionally you can drop this for a lower res latent RGB built into the node 

https://github.com/madebyollin/taehv/blob/main/safetensors/taeltx2_3.safetensors

nodes.title: Prompt Enhancer (Optional)
widgets_values: This automagically enhances your prompt using the already loaded Gemma model. But it can be a bit sensitive to having correct Gemma if using GGUF models. Alternatively you can bypass/disable  this feature

nodes.title: Custom Audio
widgets_values:
Use a reference audio instead of LTX own audio, and LTX video will lip-sync to the audio you provide. For best possible results you should prompt that the subject is talking.  And if you transcribe what is said in your audio input, results might be even better 

Mel-band RoFormer is optional for extracting a clean vocal only audio for the sampler

https://huggingface.co/Kijai/MelBandRoFormer_comfy/tree/main

folder: models\diffusion_models

nodes.title: Video settings
widgets_values:
If low on Ram/Vram, try width height to 832 x 480 or 960 x 544.  If you can, run at 1280 x 720 or higher.


Length in seconds :  try 5, 10 or 20. 
fps : 24 or 25 (or 48 or 50 if your pc can run it)

subtitles, captions, overlay graphics, overlay text, blurry, oversaturated, pixelated, low resolution, grainy, distorted, noise, compression artifacts, jpeg artifacts, glitches, watermark, text, logo, signature, copyright, distorted sound, saturated sound, loud, closed eyes, stiff face, frozen expression, static eyes, cross-eyed, wall-eyed, distorted face, deformed face, asymmetrical eyes, extra limbs, wrong hands, morphing face, face warp, identity change, different person, multiple faces, crowd, background people in focus
auto: Automatically select best available model

nodes.title: 3-Frame Keyframe Guide (LTXVAddGuide)
widgets_values:
## 🎬 3-Frame Keyframe Conditioning

Each image opens its section — the sampler interpolates between them.

**Frame 1 (Start)** — `frame_idx = 0`  
→ Uses the existing first-frame **LoadImage** at the top of the workflow.

**Frame 2 (Mid)** — `frame_idx = round(Mid Frame Time × FPS)`  
→ Upload image in **Load Mid Frame**. Set **Mid Frame Time (sec)**.

**Frame 3 (Last)** — `frame_idx = round(Last Frame Time × FPS)`  
→ Upload image in **Load Last Frame**. Set **Last Frame Time (sec)**.  
→ Video plays *from* this image until the end.

**Example** at 24 fps, 13s total: Mid=6s→frame 144, Last=10s→frame 240

Ishita, South Asian woman, dark hair in a bun, square metal-frame glasses, 
matching the reference face exactly, medium close-up, frame locked throughout.

0:00–0:02 — Extreme close-up on Ishita's right eye. Iris is sharp, 
skin texture visible at pore level. A thin horizontal red line 
sweeps left-to-right across the glass lens of her glasses, like a 
laser scan. The line flickers and duplicates into two parallel 
glitch bars, then vanishes. Her eye does not blink. Pupil slightly 
dilates. No head movement. No body movement. The frame holds 
completely still except for the glitch artifact on the lens.

0:02–0:04 — Camera pulls back smoothly to medium close-up. Ishita 
is wearing a white oversized hoodie. Her right arm extends forward, 
elbow bending outward, until her right index finger moves directly 
toward the camera lens and touches the center of the frame. At the 
moment her fingertip reaches the lens center, the entire image 
surface ripples outward from that point — concentric silver-chrome 
rings expand like a liquid mercury droplet impact, radiating edge 
to edge. The ripple is physical and wet, with metallic sheen 
catching light. Her face is visible through the distortion, 
expression neutral, eyes still locked forward.

0:04–0:05 — The silver wave moves across her body from front to 
back. The white hoodie fabric dissolves into liquid chrome droplets 
that fall downward and immediately re-solidify, rebuilding upward 
as a sculpted blazer with sharp angular shoulders and a mirror-
polished metallic surface. The texture is liquid metal — reflective, 
warping ambient color. The background simultaneously shatters into 
glass fragments that fall in slow motion and dematerialize before 
hitting the ground. Beneath her feet, a dark reflective floor 
appears, wet and mirror-like.

0:05–0:09 — Ishita stands in a high-contrast dark room. Two teal 
neon strip lights form a large glowing frame behind her. Two 
hyper-violet neon strips intersect them diagonally. The infinity 
floor reflects all neon colors in soft wavering lines. She raises 
her left hand to the bridge of her glasses and pushes them up 
slightly with one finger. Her head tilts three degrees to the right, 
slowly and deliberately. The corners of her lips move upward two 
millimeters — a controlled closed-mouth smirk. The camera micro-
drifts forward two percent into the chest area of the blazer, 
revealing the liquid-chrome texture catching teal and violet 
reflections in swirling patterns. Her hair is still. Her body is 
still except for the deliberate tilt. She holds the smirk and 
the tilt, eyes directly into camera lens.

Lighting: cold white flat light at 0:00. Hard teal and hyper-violet 
neon bilateral rim lighting from 0:05, high contrast, no fill, 
strong specular on the chrome blazer surface.

Camera: extreme close-up locked at 0:00–0:01. Smooth pull to medium 
close-up at 0:02. Locked medium close-up from 0:02 onward. Minimal 
two-percent forward drift at 0:07. No rotation. No dolly. No zoom.

Style: 4K, 24fps, cinematic realism, high saturation on neon 
elements only, skin tones natural, chrome surfaces hyper-reflective, 
extreme texture detail on fabric and skin, no motion blur except on 
the mercury ripple wave.

Audio: silent.

inputs.text: A medium close-up shot features a Caucasian man with a closely shaven head and face, wearing a black baseball cap with "PNTR" in white letters on the front, and a dark grey t-shirt with "JUST DO IT" visible across his chest. A small black microphone is clipped to his shirt collar. He is positioned slightly to the left of the frame, looking intently downwards and to his right, his eyes focused off-camera. His facial expression is one of deep concentration, with his brow slightly furrowed. As he looks down, a quick sniff sound is heard, and then he speaks with a deep male voice and a slightly frustrated tone, saying, "I think it's so bad." The camera remains static throughout, maintaining a shallow depth of field, which keeps the man in sharp focus while the background is softly blurred, revealing a light-colored wall with white wooden shelving or trim, and a partially open white wooden door on the right. After a brief pause, another short, audible sniff is heard. The man then continues to speak, his voice maintaining the same quality, as he states, "So bad. So bad." He elaborates further, emphasizing his point with a final statement, "This got to be, it's got to be the worst tool I've ever seen."
